cards.back: A method used to determine whether there is a significant difference between the sample statistics and the known population parameters.
A range of values within which a population parameter is likely to lie with a certain level of confidence.
A measure of the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values.
The average value of a set of numbers, calculated by adding up all the values and dividing by the number of values.
A set of statistical measures used to summarize and describe the basic features of a dataset, such as the mean, median, mode, and standard deviation.
